extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ attacked final kilometer uphill, gapped CARAPAZ by 13 seconds with superior acceleration despite Movistar setting tempo all climb
tactical_patterns: Movistar tempo-setting for VALVERDE/CARAPAZ backfired—MAS dropped by own team's pace, exposing flawed strength-in-numbers strategy
ROGLIČ conserved energy mid-climb while KUSS set pace for Jumbo, then attacked final kilometer when CARAPAZ committed
VALVERDE early attack from 3rd GC forced Jumbo to respond, testing rivals before decisive final kilometer move
CARAPAZ faded final 200m despite holding ROGLIČ's wheel initially—acceleration gradient separator, not sustained power

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: 13-second gap final kilometer shows devastating uphill acceleration when already at threshold on steep gradients
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: negative: dropped by ROGLIČ final 200m despite leading into finale, acceleration deficit exposed on steep finish
rider_signals.MAS Enric: negative: dropped by own Movistar's tempo despite being protected rider, concerning weakness relative to team strategy
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: paced ROGLIČ through climb before final kilometer, critical Jumbo domestique strength at altitude
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: 6 seconds off ROGLIČ pace for third, surprisingly strong on steep gradients against pure climbers
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: second place initially before fading final meters, strong positioning throughout climb
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: neutral: 33 seconds back, solid ride but gap suggests may drop from top-3 GC
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: early attack from 9th GC, aggressive tactics for Movistar but ultimately unsuccessful
rider_signals.GESINK Robert: neutral: pulled for Jumbo to control VALVERDE, critical tempo management role
